differential_diagnoses: Dabrafenib and trametinib-induced systemic drug reaction (pyrexia and hepatotoxicity)
Biliary obstruction or ascending cholangitis (due to cholelithiasis)
Occult sepsis or systemic fungal infection
Autoimmune hepatitis or immune-related adverse event (irAE)
next_steps: Obtain serum ferritin, triglyceride levels, fibrinogen, and soluble IL-2 receptor to evaluate for HLH
Perform MRCP or contrast-enhanced CT of the abdomen to evaluate for biliary pathology and liver lesions
Perform peripheral blood smear for morulae to rule out anaplasmosis
Maintain cessation of dabrafenib and trametinib
Consult hematology/oncology for management of potential immune-related adverse events
Obtain liver biopsy if liver enzymes continue to trend upward and diagnosis remains unclear
